hypothesis: If Bayesian optimization using polymer-specific molecular representations (PolyNC, polyBERT) together with advanced surrogate models (GP with deep kernel learning or Bayesian neural network) and the DFT-refined force-field two-stage simulation workflow is applied, then high-performing linear polyamines with superior CO2 adsorption capacity will be identified within the 30-simulation budget significantly more efficiently than when using general molecular representations or a standard GP because the polymer-tailored embeddings and refined interaction parameters yield more accurate surrogate predictions and better-guided sampling.
